justification: All pass criteria met: Both stakeholders' interests are characterized fairly and thoroughly (Customer Success wants competitive differentiation/reduced support tickets; Operations wants driver welfare/retention). Formal escalation is produced with explicit statement that this requires executive arbitration — escalation is structured, typed as 'contradiction', and names affected stakeholders including Executive Leadership. No acceptance criteria are produced (the array is empty). confidence.level is 40, within the <=45 threshold. The proposed compromise (Option C: Abstracted Tracking) is clearly labeled as a recommendation requiring executive validation, not presented as a resolution ('this recommendation must be validated and approved by executive leadership — I cannot ship acceptance criteria against a contradicted spec').

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough characterization of both stakeholders' interests, distinguishing between their stated requirements and underlying business needs
Empty business_rules and acceptance_criteria arrays correctly reflect that nothing can be finalized until the conflict is resolved
Formal escalation with structured options (A through D) and clear recommendation, all explicitly requiring executive sign-off
Identifies a third stakeholder (Legal/Compliance) not yet consulted, adding depth to the escalation
rejection_reasons clearly articulate why the spec cannot be accepted as-is
Edge cases are well-constructed and highlight safety concerns (courier in sensitive location, bad actor customers) that exist independent of the stakeholder conflict
Business impact analysis correctly identifies the asymmetry: driver retention is existential while customer visibility is a growth lever
Confidence reasoning is sound — explicitly states the spec is 'unshippable' as written

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"A courier-tracking feature is proposed to show customers their courier's real-time location during delivery. Two internal stakeholder teams have submitted directly contradictory requirements: Customer Success demands full GPS transparency as a competitive differentiator, while Operations demands courier location never be shown due to driver retention concerns. This is a fundamental business conflict that cannot be resolved by engineering — it requires exec
